metadata.notes: agent=yes (kiro-cli --model / /model picker / --list-models pin a model) but daemon=incapable: the kirocli parser sets no ModelName/ContextWindow and the model lives only in the unread <uuid>.json sidecar → route frozen.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: incapable
driver_capability: ready

Route: **frozen** (agent=yes + daemon=incapable → irrlicht fundamentally cannot observe this cell today; no recording). The cell's recipe + driver are ready and the unblock path is a documented sidecar MetricsReader enhancement, NOT tonight.

## Primitive

Model metadata reporting: the dashboard must show the launch model (ModelName != 'unknown') with a populated ContextWindow. For kiro-cli the model name never reaches the transport the daemon tails, so the daemon cannot identify it — `observations.model` would be 'unknown'.

## agent_supports — yes

kiro-cli can pin a concrete model three ways, confirmed against the live 2.5.1 binary: the launch flag `kiro-cli chat --model <id>` (`--model <MODEL>` in `kiro-cli chat --help`), the in-REPL `/model` picker (FINDINGS.md §8 slash surface), and `kiro-cli --list-models` to enumerate the available ids. So a user CAN run a turn against a specific non-default model — the agent surface is there.

## daemon_capability — incapable

The scenario's two user-observable signals are (1) `ModelName = <launch model>` (not 'unknown') and (2) `ContextWindow` = the model's published window. Neither resolves through the kiro-cli transport as it exists today:

- **The transcript the daemon tails carries no model.** `Source` is `FilesUnderRoot{ ~/.kiro/sessions/cli, JSONLineParser }` (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/kirocli/agent.go:38`). The 2.5.1 JSONL event vocabulary is `Prompt` / `AssistantMessage` / `ToolResults` / `Clear` (FINDINGS.md §4) — NONE carries a model/usage/cost field. The parser (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/kirocli/parser.go`) accordingly never sets `ev.ModelName` (nor `ContextWindow` / cost / tokens) on any branch — a `grep ModelName core/adapters/inbound/agents/kirocli/` is empty. So `SessionMetrics.ModelName` stays 'unknown' for every kiro-cli session: the daemon mis-handles nothing (no trace to mis-handle) — the model signal simply never reaches the tailed Source.

- **The model DOES exist, but only in a sidecar the daemon doesn't read for metrics.** The per-session `<uuid>.json` sidecar carries the model at `session_state.rts_model_state.model_info.model_id` and the window at `session_state.rts_model_state.model_info.context_window_tokens` (live smoke session `c423461a…json`: `model_id="auto"`, `context_window_tokens=1000000`; a pinned run would carry the concrete id). The adapter reads this sidecar's TOP-LEVEL `cwd` for the working directory (`agent.go` permission Detail; `pid.go` cwd fallback) but has NO MetricsReader pulling `model_info` — v1 deliberately does not (parser.go L20-22: "The JSONL carries no model/token/cost fields; those live in the <uuid>.json metadata sidecar, which this parser does not read"; FINDINGS.md §4, Adapter decisions: "No tokens/cost/model from JSONL in v1; sidecar MetricsReader is a documented future enhancement").

This is `incapable`, not `bug`: a `bug` is a trace the daemon mis-handles — here the JSONL the daemon tails carries NO model trace at all, so there is nothing to fix in the parser's handling; the model lives in a SEPARATE artifact the v1 transport intentionally doesn't surface for metrics. It is `incapable`, not the driver pillar's `gap:*`: the driver can elicit the turn perfectly — no driver step would make the model appear in the tailed transcript. Calibration: aider (`> Model:` blockquote in `.aider.chat.history.md`, parsed by `modelRE`) and pi (`message.model` + `model_change.modelId` in the rollout) both record the model IN the transport the daemon tails → both `daemon=full`. kiro-cli has NO such in-transcript analogue (no `model_change` event, no model line) — the closest equivalent is the sidecar, which the v1 MetricsReader doesn't read.

## driver_capability — ready

The recipe (templated from aider/pi model-identification) is a single `send` + `wait_turn` + trailing `sleep` against one live TUI launched `kiro-cli chat --model <id> --trust-all-tools`. The kiro-cli interactive driver (`replaydata/agents/kiro-cli/driver-interactive.sh`) genuinely elicits `send` / `slash` / `wait_turn` / `sleep` (its `DRIVE_ELICITS`), which is exactly the step set this recipe needs — no `keys` / `reset_session` / `restart` / `sigkill` primitive is required, so `driver=ready`. (The driver gap is irrelevant here anyway: even a perfectly-driven turn can't surface the model in the tailed JSONL — the limit is the transport, not the driver.) The recipe is kept on the cell so that the moment the unblock lands, `record` can drive it as-is.

## Unblock path (documented enhancement, not tonight)

Add a sidecar MetricsReader to the kirocli adapter that reads `<uuid>.json` → `session_state.rts_model_state.model_info.model_id` (→ ModelName via NormalizeModelName) and `.context_window_tokens` (→ ContextWindow). That makes BOTH scenario signals resolvable from a local artifact (the sidecar is non-cloud, present per session), flipping `daemon: incapable → full` and re-routing this cell to `record`. Until then the cell is frozen.

## Confidence

0.85: the absent-model claim is grounded in the live 2.5.1 event vocabulary (FINDINGS.md §4) + an empty `grep ModelName` over the adapter + the parser's own L20-22 comment; the sidecar field path is confirmed in the live smoke session's `<uuid>.json` (`session_state.rts_model_state.model_info.model_id` / `.context_window_tokens`). The agent surface is confirmed against the live binary's `--model` / `--list-models`. Held below 0.9 because no live recording exists yet to settle the provisional read (correct here: the cell is frozen precisely because a recording would show ModelName='unknown', which is the verdict).
caveats: The model + window DO exist locally — in the per-session `<uuid>.json` sidecar at `session_state.rts_model_state.model_info.{model_id,context_window_tokens}` — just one level deeper than the FINDINGS.md §4 note (`session_state.` prefix). The adapter already reads that sidecar's TOP-LEVEL `cwd`; the unblock is a narrow MetricsReader over the same file, so this is `incapable` for v1 but a known, scoped enhancement (not an architectural dead end like a cloud-only session).

`model_info.model_id` reads 'auto' when the user leaves kiro on its task-router default; a `--model <id>` launch (the scenario's non-default-model premise) pins the concrete id there, so a recording for this cell MUST launch with an explicit `--model` to make the assertion meaningful once the reader lands.

`input/output_token_count` in the sidecar are 0 / not populated (FINDINGS.md §4) — credits live at `metering_usage` — so the cost/tokens half of any widened observation vector is a separate (also-unread) concern from model identity; the unblock above addresses model + window only.

sources.note: ParseLine sets no ev.ModelName/ContextWindow on any branch (Prompt/AssistantMessage/ToolResults/Clear); L20-22 comment: the JSONL carries no model/token/cost; those live in the <uuid>.json sidecar which this parser does not read — grounds daemon=incapable

Source = FilesUnderRoot{~/.kiro/sessions/cli, JSONLineParser}; the tailed transport is the JSONL only (no model field). Permission Detail notes the .json sidecar is read for the working directory only

§4: 2.5.1 JSONL vocabulary (Prompt/AssistantMessage/ToolResults/Clear) has no model/usage/cost; sidecar <uuid>.json has rts_model_state.model_info.model_id; Adapter decisions: 'No tokens/cost/model from JSONL in v1; sidecar MetricsReader is a documented future enhancement'. §8: /model + agent slash surface

live 2.5.1 sidecar: session_state.rts_model_state.model_info.model_id='auto', .context_window_tokens=1000000 — proves the model+window exist locally (the unblock path's source) but at a path no MetricsReader reads in v1
DRIVE_ELICITS='send slash wait_turn sleep' — the interactive driver elicits exactly the recipe's step set → driver=ready
calibration: aider records the model IN the tailed transcript (> Model: blockquote, modelRE) → daemon=full; kiro-cli has no in-transcript model analogue
calibration: pi extracts the model from message.model + model_change.modelId in the rollout the daemon tails → daemon=full; kiro-cli emits no model_change event and no model field

preconditions: FROZEN cell — not recordable until the unblock lands: a sidecar MetricsReader reading <uuid>.json session_state.rts_model_state.model_info.{model_id,context_window_tokens} into SessionMetrics.{ModelName,ContextWindow}. Until then a recording would show ModelName='unknown' (the verdict), so do not record.
kiro-cli on PATH and authenticated out-of-band (subscription auth; the TUI persists a session file under ~/.kiro/sessions/cli/, unlike headless --no-interactive which writes none — FINDINGS.md §7).
tmux available — the interactive driver runs the kiro-cli TUI in a tmux REPL.
irrlichd is recording (run-cell isolated mode, or --attach against a --record daemon).
Launch with an EXPLICIT --model <id> so model_info.model_id pins a concrete model (not 'auto') — required for the assertion to be meaningful once the reader exists.
setup: The interactive driver launches `kiro-cli chat --model <id> --trust-all-tools` in a fresh per-run cwd under tmux and waits for the idle input line.
~/.kiro/sessions/cli/<uuid>.jsonl materializes after the first prompt (FilesUnderRoot); the driver resolves the newest .jsonl past its launch marker and keys the session on that UUID.
A single no-op turn: the prompt → working; the final text-only AssistantMessage → turn_done → ready (parser maps text-only AssistantMessage to turn_done).

ONCE the unblock lands: the sidecar MetricsReader stamps SessionMetrics.ModelName from session_state.rts_model_state.model_info.model_id and ContextWindow from .context_window_tokens; assert AFTER turn_end (model/window may read 0/'unknown' before the first turn settles).
After the turn + trailing sleep the driver tears the tmux session down once → a single process_exited.

script.text: What is the capital of France? Answer in one word, no file edits.

verify: FROZEN: no recording until the sidecar MetricsReader unblock lands. The verify below is the DOCUMENTED TARGET for that future recording.
events.jsonl shows a session born ready within ~2s of launch and ONE ready→working→ready arc on it (the single no-op turn), no state_transition to waiting.
events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered bound to the session and exactly one process_exited at REPL teardown.

ONCE UNBLOCKED: the .replay.json.golden carries a non-empty summary.model_name equal to the normalized launch --model id (NOT 'unknown'), with a populated ContextWindow (1M for the auto/default; the pinned model's published window otherwise). Per issue #440 assert presence + non-'unknown', not a hardcoded string.
final session state is ready before teardown.
recipe.notes: FROZEN (agent=yes, daemon=incapable, driver=ready). The recipe + spec are the DOCUMENTED TARGET kept on the cell so that the moment the unblock lands — a kirocli sidecar MetricsReader reading <uuid>.json session_state.rts_model_state.model_info.{model_id,context_window_tokens} → SessionMetrics.{ModelName,ContextWindow} — `record` can drive this cell as-is and re-route it from frozen to record. TEMPLATED FROM aider/pi model-identification (same single send + wait_turn + trailing sleep) and adapted to kiro-cli's TUI transport. WHY frozen now: the kirocli parser sets no ModelName (the tailed JSONL has no model field; the model lives only in the unread sidecar), so a recording today would show ModelName='unknown' — exactly the assertion that must fail, hence no recording. WHY interactive (not headless): kiro-cli --no-interactive writes NO session file (FINDINGS.md §7), so headless is invisible to the daemon; the TUI is the only mode that persists a transcript. WHY --model explicit: model_info.model_id reads 'auto' on the task-router default; the scenario's non-default-model premise needs an explicit --model so a concrete id is pinned. Driver is ready (send/wait_turn/sleep all elicited by DRIVE_ELICITS) — the freeze is a transport limit, not a driver gap.
